acquisition method: the next-generation proteomics platform, DIA (data-independent acquisition)
alkylation reagent: 10 mM CAA and 100 mM TrisHCl pH 8.5
alkylation concentration: 10 mM CAA and 100 mM TrisHCl pH 8.5
cleavage agent: adding LysC and trypsin in a 1:50 ratio
collision energy: HCD fragmentation was applied with 27% collision energy
instrument: injected on a Thermo Scientific™ Orbitrap Fusion™ Tribrid™ mass spectrometer
labeling: TMT (Tandem Mass Tagging)-based proteomic quantification method
